$comment: D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY (plan 24 Stage 6) end-to-end witness for the now-iterative CONST-EVALUATORS. There are TWO: the HIR const-evaluator `evaluateConstant` (src/hir/const_eval.cpp — folds a global initializer / sizeof / enum value) and the CST const-evaluator `evaluateConstantCst` (src/hir/cst_const_eval.cpp — folds a CST const-expr such as an array dimension, BEFORE HIR exists). Both are now EXPLICIT WORK-STACK DRIVERS for the deep STRAIGHT-LINE arms (HIR: BinaryOp / UnaryOp / Cast; CST: plain-binary / unary / the transparent WRAPPER-paren descent — the arms whose only recursion is into a child sub-expression), so a deeply-nested const-expression folds with FLAT O(1) host-stack cost per nesting level instead of one host-recursion frame per level. This program exercises BOTH: the global `int g`'s initializer is `((( ... 150 nested parens ... (10 - 4) ... )))` = 6, folded by the HIR evaluator (deep WRAPPER + plain-binary frames) at HIR->MIR globals lowering; the array `int a[((( ... 150 nested parens ... (8 + 1) ... )))]` has dimension 9 (so `sizeof(a)` == 9 * sizeof(int) == 36), folded by the CST evaluator (deep WRAPPER + plain-binary frames) at semantic Pass 1. `main` returns `g + (int)sizeof(a)` == 6 + 36 == 42. The value 42 is miscompile-sensitive: a dropped/duplicated paren-descent level, a wrong binary combine (10 - 4 / 8 + 1), or a wrong-phase work-stack pop would shift either fold and change the exit code. 150 nesting levels is far past the host main-stack crash point (~25 levels for the heavy recursive fold frame) and ~12x the deepest const-expr in the rest of the corpus; a regression that UNWRAPPED either evaluator's deep arm back to host recursion would recurse 150 frames deep during the fold. The whole front-end build (semantic + CST->HIR + HIR->MIR) runs on the 64 MiB worker stack (core/substrate/large_stack_call), so 150 levels is within budget either way — this example is the deep-flatten + real-codegen RUN/correctness witness (the flattened evaluators produce correct values through the FULL pipeline); the strict byte-identity-at-depth witnesses for the HIR evaluator live in the unit pins IterativeDeepBinaryChainFoldsFlatAndByteIdentical / IterativeDeepUnaryChainFoldsFlatAndByteIdentical / IterativeDeepCastChainFoldsFlatAndByteIdentical (3000-deep, in tests/hir/test_const_eval.cpp), and OUTPUT-IDENTITY against the prior recursive evaluators is enforced by the full ctest suite (the array-dimension / global-init / sizeof / enum goldens stay unchanged). The optimizedPipelines arm proves the lowered MIR survives the full release pipeline (ConstFold + the rest collapse the folded constants; the baseline arm is the real-codegen witness). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
